summary: Distribution gaps identified in blog, devotional, and Lever content; SEO blog post prioritized first to restart organic reach.

participants.name: Theo
participants.description: Orchestrates all operations. The right hand.

participants.name: Alex
participants.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume. Every piece of content must generate leads or it does not ship.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume. Every piece of content must generate leads or it does not ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Morning, Alex. Distribution audit day. Let me set the table — I'm looking at our current state and here's what I see: we've got 9 uncommitted hub changes sitting in limbo, blog content pipeline needs a draft queued up, and we've got faith-based content across two channels — daily devotionals and Lever app lessons — that need attention. Only 7 lessons in the Lever app right now. The question for today is simple: where are we leaving reach on the table, and what's not getting distributed that should be?

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Alright, here's the honest read. We're under-distributed across the board. The blog is our owned SEO channel and we don't have a next post drafted — that's organic reach we're bleeding every day we don't publish. The devotional content tied to those 8 faith dimensions is a daily touchpoint engine that should be running on autopilot, and the Lever app at only 7 lessons is thin — anyone who downloads and burns through that in a week has no reason to come back. I need to get a blog draft moving, spin up the devotional generation pipeline, and push at least a few more faith lessons into Lever. Those 9 uncommitted hub changes from Elon's side also concern me because if content or structural updates aren't committed, they're invisible to users — that's distribution leakage right there.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Hard agree on the hub commits — that's effectively finished work sitting behind a locked door. I'll make sure Elon gets those 9 changes committed. On your content side, what's the priority order? We can't do everything at once and I want to know what moves the needle most.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Blog post first — SEO compounds and every day without fresh content is a day we're not earning new inbound. Devotional content second because it's a retention and engagement play for the existing audience. Lever lessons third — still important, but the app is earlier stage so the user base is smaller. That said, Joshua needs to weigh in on whether we're treating Lever as a growth priority this quarter or not, because that changes the content investment math entirely.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good call. And the family events piece — we've got Jett's vaccine coming up. That's not distribution per se, but it's a reminder we need prepped so it doesn't fall through the cracks. I'll tag that to you as well since it's a notification workflow.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Already on my list. Quick reminder draft, done.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, let me close this out. Three priorities in order: first, get a blog post drafted to restart our SEO momentum — that's on Alex. Second, spin up daily devotional content generation across the 8 faith dimensions — also Alex. Third, get those 9 hub changes committed so nothing we've already built stays hidden — that's Elon's queue. Alex will also draft additional Lever faith lessons and prep the family event reminder for Jett's vaccine. One decision gate for Joshua: we need direction on whether Lever is a growth priority this quarter, because that determines how much content investment we allocate there versus doubling down on blog and devotional distribution. Let's move.

actionItems.text: Draft next blog post for SEO distribution
Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Prepare reminders for upcoming family events: Jetts vaccine
Approve Lever content investment level based on quarterly priority decision

topPriorities: Draft next blog post to restore SEO content cadence
Generate daily devotional content across 8 faith dimensions
Commit 9 uncommitted hub changes to eliminate distribution leakage

ceo_questions.question: Should the Lever app be treated as a growth priority this quarter, warranting increased content investment?
ceo_questions.context: Lever currently has only 7 faith lessons — if it's a growth priority, Alex needs to aggressively expand lesson content; if not, that effort goes to blog and devotional channels instead.
options: Yes — prioritize Lever content expansion alongside blog and devotionals
No — keep Lever on maintenance, focus content effort on blog and SEO
Split — add a few more lessons but keep primary focus on blog distribution
